claims.claim: GLM-5.2 scores 51 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1, the top open-weights model and roughly 5th-6th overall, ~4-5 points behind Opus 4.8 (56) and GPT-5.5 (55).
GLM-5.2 is a 753B-total / ~40B-active Mixture-of-Experts model (256 routed + 1 shared experts, 8 active), 1M-token context, MIT-licensed, released ~2026-06-13, and text-only.
Anthropic's Opus 4.8 and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 disclose only interface specs (1M / 1.05M context, 128k output, modalities, cutoff, price) and zero architecture/parameter information.
On GDPval-AA v2 (real-world economic tasks), GLM-5.2 scores 1524, edging GPT-5.5 (xhigh) at 1514.
GLM-5.2 burns ~43,000 output tokens per Intelligence Index task (~37k of it reasoning), the most verbose model in its class (MiniMax-M3 24k, Kimi K2.6 35k, DeepSeek V4 Pro 37k).
API list price: GLM-5.2 $1.40 in / $4.40 out per 1M tokens; Opus 4.8 $5 / $25; GPT-5.5 $5 / $30 — making GLM ~5.7-6.8x cheaper per output token.

Running the full AA Intelligence Index cost $982.90 for GLM-5.2 (140M tokens) vs $2,852.88 for GPT-5.5 xhigh (72M) and $4,011.58 for Opus 4.8 (120M), shrinking GLM's real cost advantage to ~3x vs GPT-5.5 and ~4x vs Opus.
On the independent Vals AI SWE-bench Verified run, Opus 4.8 scores 88.60% and GPT-5.5 82.60%; GLM-5.2 has no independent SWE-bench Verified entry.

GLM-5.2's headline 'beats GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding' claim rests on FrontierSWE 74.4 vs 72.6, a ~1.8-point vendor-reported margin with no independent replication; on the same vendor table Opus 4.8 (75.1) still leads GLM-5.2.
On Humanity's Last Exam without tools, Opus 4.8 scores 49.8% and GPT-5.5 41.4%, while GLM-5.2 reaches ~40% (AA-measured) — a ~10-point gap to Opus on the discriminating reasoning benchmark.
On FrontierMath Tiers 1-3, GPT-5.5 (xhigh) scores 85.3% and Opus 4.8 80.0%; GLM-5.2 has no independently published FrontierMath score.
AIME and GPQA Diamond are saturated (all three models score 89-100%), so apparent parity there is uninformative; GLM-5.2's vendor GPQA 91.2% drops to 89% when measured independently by AA.
On GLM's own model card, GLM-5.2 trails Opus 4.8 on long-horizon agentic tasks: SWE-Marathon 13.0 vs 26.0 (a 2x gap), Toolathlon 48.2 vs 59.9, MCP-Atlas 76.8 vs 77.8.
GLM-5.2 is text-only, while GPT-5.5 is natively multimodal/omnimodal and Opus 4.8 accepts image and PDF input; GLM vision exists only via the separate, weaker GLM-V line.
On AA-Omniscience (closed-book factuality), GLM-5.2 scores index 4 (25.1% accuracy) vs Opus 4.8's index 27 (46.6% accuracy) — far weaker factual reliability.

GLM-family models exhibit heavy political censorship: GLM-5 scored ~3% compliance on ChinaBench's politically sensitive prompts, and peer-reviewed work finds China-origin models refuse more and answer less accurately on political questions.

Serving GLM-5.2 requires ~750GB VRAM at FP8 (8x H200-class GPUs) or ~372GB at INT4 (4x H200); break-even vs z.ai's API is roughly 2.4B output tokens/month (~3,000+ prompts/day), so self-hosting is an enterprise play, not an individual cost saver.

On blind human-preference frontend coding (Code Arena: Frontend), GLM-5.2 ranks #2 at ~1595 Elo, behind only Fable 5 (1654) and ahead of Opus 4.8 (1561); it is #1 on DesignArena (1360) but only #25 on Text Arena.
DeepSeek V4 Pro is also open-weight (MIT), has vision, and is ~5x cheaper than GLM-5.2 on output ($0.87 vs $4.40 per 1M), but scores lower on the AA Index (44 vs 51).

Open MIT weights give buyers what a closed API structurally cannot: fine-tuning/distillation rights (cf. the open Nemotron post-trained into the frontier band on Harvey's benchmark), deprecation immunity, air-gap deployment, and freedom from silent model swaps such as OpenAI's GPT-5 router incident.

GLM-5.2 launched on 2026-06-13 and as of 2026-06-22 is too new to appear in OpenRouter's weekly top-10 by tokens, which is led by DeepSeek V4 Flash (~10.9T tokens); Anthropic earns ~42% of OpenRouter revenue on ~11% of tokens.
